message:
fix(encoding,decoding): validate object params layout and source block K

Two correctness improvements to the encoding/decoding pipeline:

Encoding:
- New validate_source_block_k(): checks each block's K against
  SystematicParams::try_for_source_block() before encoding begins.
  Previously, unsupported K values (e.g., K=56404 for a 451KB block
  with symbol_size=8) would only fail deep inside the systematic
  encoder with an opaque error.

Decoding:
- New validate_object_params_layout(): cross-checks declared
  source_blocks and symbols_per_block against the computed block plans.
  Rejects mismatches early before latching object identity, preventing
  a poisoned decoder state on malformed metadata.
- set_object_params() now validates before setting object_id/object_size,
  so a failed validation doesn't leave the pipeline half-initialized.
- required_symbols(): distinguishes NaN (→ minimum_threshold) from
  +Infinity (→ usize::MAX) instead of collapsing both into
  minimum_threshold.
- New sum_required_symbols() helper deduplicates the fold-over-plans
  pattern used in progress() and into_data().
- symbols_per_block in test roundtrips changed from total_k to
  full_block_k (max per-block K), matching the actual wire format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
